user.institution: Manipal Hospital HAL Airport Road Bangalore

questions.question_text: lndwelling catheters are designed to:

options.option_text: Use for straight cath to obtain a urine specimen
Use for a patient who is incontinent
Has no balloon for securing in the bladder
Rest securely in the bladder for a limited amount of time.

selected_options.selected_option_text: Use for a patient who is incontinent

questions.question_text: In the procedure for inserting an indwelling catheter, after urine appears in the tubing, the catheter is advanced additional 2-inches in order to:

options.option_text: Minimize the risk for balloon inflation in the urethra.
Increase the amount of urine drained.
Stimulate normal bladder contraction.
Promote Bladder Tone

selected_options.selected_option_text: Minimize the risk for balloon inflation in the urethra.

questions.question_text: Why are there two lumens in a Foley catheter?

options.option_text: One to drain urine and other to inflate balloon
One to drain urine and other to administer medication
One to administer medication and other to inflate balloon
Both to drain urine

selected_options.selected_option_text: One to drain urine and other to inflate balloon

questions.question_text: The catheter slips into the vagina during a straight catheterization of a female patient. Which action by the nurse is appropriate?

options.option_text: Removes the catheter and redirects it to the urinary meatus
Removes the catheter, wipes it with a sterile gauze and redirects it to the urinary meatus
Leaves the catheter in place and gets a new sterile catheter
Leaves the catheter in place and asks another nurse to attempt the procedure

selected_options.selected_option_text: Removes the catheter, wipes it with a sterile gauze and redirects it to the urinary meatus
Leaves the catheter in place and gets a new sterile catheter

questions.question_text: How many centimeters should the urinary catheter be advanced in a male?

options.option_text: 5-6 cm
2-5 cm

selected_options.selected_option_text: 2-5 cm

questions.question_text: What is the size of urinary catheter used in children?

options.option_text: 18 Fr

questions.question_text: How long can a silicon catheter stay in situ?

options.option_text: 2 weeks
12 weeks
3 weeks
20 weeks

selected_options.selected_option_text: 12 weeks

questions.question_text: State if True or False. Catheter-associated urinary tract infections are the most common cause of bacteremia (bloodstream infection) in long-term care facilities.

options.option_text: True
False

selected_options.selected_option_text: False

questions.question_text: State if True or False. During catheter care, gloves only need to be worn if you are emptying the urine drainage bag.

options.option_text: True
False

selected_options.selected_option_text: False

questions.question_text: Why should a catheter drainage bag be kept below the level of the bladder?

options.option_text: It prevents urine from flowing back into the bladder and causing an infection
The bag is likely to leak if it is held higher than the bladder
The tubing can disconnect from the catheter if it is kept higher than the level of the bladder
The tubing may kink and cause safety hazard to the patient

selected_options.selected_option_text: It prevents urine from flowing back into the bladder and causing an infection

questions.question_text: How should a drainage bag be emptied?

options.option_text: Into a pitcher and poured down the sink
Into a measuring jug and then poured into the toilet
Drained directly into a 24-hr specimen container
Disconnected from the catheter tubing and released directly into the toilet

selected_options.selected_option_text: Into a measuring jug and then poured into the toilet

questions.question_text: Urinary catheterization involves inserting the Foley's catheter

options.option_text: Into the ureter
Through the urethra into the bladder
Through the rectum
From ureter into the bladder

selected_options.selected_option_text: Through the urethra into the bladder

questions.question_text: All the statements about urinary catheterization are true except

options.option_text: It relieves retention of urine
It is not an invasive procedure
It helps obtain urine sample
It helps in monitoring the urinary output

selected_options.selected_option_text: It is not an invasive procedure

questions.question_text: Which out of the following in the given table is incorrect: (I. Male - 13-18 Fr | II. Female - 12-16 Fr | III. Hematuria - <18 Fr | IV. Children - 5-12 Fr | V. Infants - Feeding tube less than 5 Fr)

options.option_text: I and II
I and III
IV
III and V

selected_options.selected_option_text: IV

questions.question_text: In a female patient with indwelling catheter the catheter care should be performed in which of the following correct sequence

options.option_text: Urinary meatus, labia minora, labia majora, catheter
Catheter, urinary meatus, labia majora, labia minora
Urobag, catheter, labia majora
Labia minora, labia majora, perineum, catheter

selected_options.selected_option_text: Labia minora, labia majora, perineum, catheter

questions.question_text: The primary reason for taping an indwelling catheter laterally to the thigh of a male client is to:

options.option_text: Eliminate pressure at the penoscrotal angle.
Prevent the catheter from kinking in the urethra.
Prevent accidental catheter removal.
Allow the client to turn without kinking the catheter.

selected_options.selected_option_text: Prevent accidental catheter removal.

questions.question_text: The primary reason for lubricating the urinary catheter generously before inserting the catheter into a male client is that this technique helps reduce:

options.option_text: Spasms at the orifice of the bladder.
Friction along the urethra when the catheter is being inserted.
The number of organisms gaining entrance to the bladder.
The formation of encrustations that may occur at the end of the catheter.

selected_options.selected_option_text: Friction along the urethra when the catheter is being inserted.

questions.question_text: A nurse is caring for a client with an indwelling urinary catheter. What is the most important action for the nurse to implement when irrigating the bladder?

options.option_text: Use sterile equipment.
Instill the fluid under high pressure.
Warm the solution to body temperature.
Aspirate immediately to ensure return flow.

selected_options.selected_option_text: Use sterile equipment.

questions.question_text: A urine specimen is needed to test for the presence of ketones in a client who is diabetic. What should the nurse do when collecting this specimen from a urinary retention catheter?

options.option_text: Disconnect the catheter and drain the urine into a clean container.
Clean the drainage valve and remove the urine from the catheter bag.
Wipe the catheter with alcohol and drain the urine into a sterile test tube.
Clamp the catheter, cleanse the port, and use a sterile syringe to remove urine.

selected_options.selected_option_text: Clamp the catheter, cleanse the port, and use a sterile syringe to remove urine.

questions.question_text: Perform the procedure in the correct sequence.

options.option_text: Check patient identification with name and Hosp No
Explained the procedure to the patient to gain co-operation. Assembled the articles near the bedside
Provided privacy. Washed hands & put on sterile gloves
Cleaned the perineum using antiseptic lotion with forceps. One stroke from up to down with one gauze
Opened outer plastic cover of the catheter in such a way that inner cover is not contaminated and slide it into the catheterization pack
Instructed patient to take a deep breath and relax. Inserted the catheter about 5 to 7.5cms
If retention catheter is required, introduced sterile distilled water into inflate the balloon
Pulled the catheter slightly outward to ascertain stability
Connected the urobag for continuous urinary drainage and kept below the level of bladder
Checked the colour of the urine. Make the patient comfortable and replaced the equipment
Recorded the colour, amount and any abnormality of urine

selected_sequence.selected_option_text: Check patient identification with name and Hosp No
Explained the procedure to the patient to gain co-operation. Assembled the articles near the bedside
Provided privacy. Washed hands & put on sterile gloves
Cleaned the perineum using antiseptic lotion with forceps. One stroke from up to down with one gauze
Opened outer plastic cover of the catheter in such a way that inner cover is not contaminated and slide it into the catheterization pack
Instructed patient to take a deep breath and relax. Inserted the catheter about 5 to 7.5cms
If retention catheter is required, introduced sterile distilled water into inflate the balloon
Pulled the catheter slightly outward to ascertain stability
Connected the urobag for continuous urinary drainage and kept below the level of bladder
Checked the colour of the urine. Make the patient comfortable and replaced the equipment
Recorded the colour, amount and any abnormality of urine
